name: ExtensionTransport_Requester
title: R5: Who is asking for transport to be done (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Transport.requester` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Transport.requester` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Device,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Organization,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Practitioner,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/PractitionerRole,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/RelatedPerson)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `Transport.requester` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Device,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Organization,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Practitioner,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/PractitionerRole,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/RelatedPerson)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Transport.requester` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Transport` is not mapped.
Element `Transport.requester` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
